title: Nile Owen
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Owen}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:NileOwen-CA.png]] |name=Nile Owen |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[The Abduction]]''; republished as {{WEGCite|book=Classic Adventures|story=The Abduction|stext=The Abduction of Crying Dawn Singer}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Learn what you can from him. Take what is left, and execute him. Send his family an expense voucher for the procedure.|Moff Nile Owen|}} [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] '''Nile Owen''' was an ambitious and talented [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] officer who served as [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He liked to use furred cloaks with his Imperial uniform. Threatening, intelligent and cultured, Owen also enraged easily and [[Death/Legends|executed]] anyone failing him via either betrayal or ineptitude. As such, he killed his assistant [[Nak Farool]] when he discovered Farool was conspiring to oust him. He also killed a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] when they failed to bring him live [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] to question. ==Biography== {{Quote|The plan did not include vilifying me. Admiral, have the subspace relay station destroyed.''"
"''Yes, your Excellency.''"
"''And have this place cleaned up.|Moff Owen and Admiral Sahreel, during Nak Farool's execution|}} Joining the military forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Nile Owen served aboard several older [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]]. His distinguished service, coupled with his skill at making influential friends, led him to rapid, regular promotion. During his advance, Owen also [[Parent/Legends|fathered]] [[Nile Owen's daughter|one daughter]].''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' Eventually becoming the [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], or [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Governor/Legends|governor]], of the [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]], in charge of its stability, Owen still worked hard: He wanted to win control of another, more important sector. Soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] began diplomatic talks with the [[Shashay]] [[Species/Legends|species]], hoping to convince the Shashay to join the Alliance and to allow a Rebel base in their hidden [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Crytal Nest]]. This was opposite the interests of the Empire. The Empire also discovered that [[Crying Dawn Singer]], a Shashay cultural hero who was to perform for Owen in the opening of the [[Sector Performing Arts Center]], had a Rebel undercover agent in his entourage. Owen's Special Assistant [[Nak Farool]] convinced the Moff to approve a [[Operation 45RA|mission]]: The Empire would secretly hire a criminal to kidnap Crying Dawn Singer in an operation that would lead to the discovery of the [[Spy/Legends|spy]] and, hopefully, of a Rebel base. Later, the criminal would publicly make unacceptable ransom demands while falsely claiming to be a leader of the Alliance. This would hamper the Alliance's advances and cause the Shashay to turn to the Empire. Hesitantly accepting, Owen did not know that Farool planned to betray him: Farool would broadcast not only the ransom demand, but also other transmission claiming that Owen was uninterested in the abduction. This would undermine Owen's popularity. Besides, when Farool hired the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Yearo Seville]] to do the dirty job, he told Seville to deliver the hostage not to Owen, but to Farool's brother [[Braig Farool|Braig]], Governor of the planet [[Narg]]. Braig Farool would later feign a rescue operation and "recover" Crying Dawn Singer, gaining prestige. Following the conspiracy, Owen would be forced to resign, and Braig Farool would replace him as Moff. Seville abducted Crying Dawn Singer and his entourage, and took them to an abandoned Imperial base on the planet [[Najarka]]. There, [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] discovered that the secret agent was the Shashay's [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[KL-6TLF7V/T]]. Owen was informed about this, and he set off for Najarka aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Impending Doom]]'', as he wanted to recover the droid. Owen's staff intended to analyze the droid's memory. Seville left with the Shashay for the [[subspace relay station]] at [[Laim system/Legends|Laim]], where he would broadcast the ransom demands, leaving the droid behind as ordered. {{RPG|''[[The Abduction]]''}} However, once Owen reached Najarka, he was surprised to discover a team of Rebel agents trying to leave with KL-6TLF7V/T. The Moff was unable to prevent their escape, even if he deployed a [[Telgorn Corporation/Legends|Telgorn]] [[Gamma-class Light Personnel Carrier|personal carrier]] with [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and four [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. Seville broadcast his ransom notice from Laim, and the Shashay believed his lies. Shashay [[Navigator/Legends|navigators]] in Rebel [[Starship/Legends|starships]] hijacked their vehicles and threatened to ask Owen for help if Crying Dawn Singer was not returned quickly. Owen went to Laim in the ''Impeding Doom''. Investigating with his subordinate [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Sahreel]], the Moff discovered a recorded message of Nak Farool vilifying Owen. Furious, he ordered Farool arrested and personally executed him in the ship's bridge, while at the same time ordered the relay station destroyed. Owen followed Farool's track to Narg and sent the Imperial [[Cruiser/Legends|Cruiser]] ''[[Kana's Steel]]'', under [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Williar Barrog]], to take control of the local [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. The ''Impending Doom'' followed shortly afterward. When Owen arrived at Narg, the Rebel team he had seen on Najarka was already there, trying to rescue Crying Dawn Singer from Braig Farool's clutches. Owen intended to personally recover Crying Dawn Singer, restoring his damaged reputation. However, even if he sent a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of stormtroopers against Farool's corporate guards, Owen failed: The Rebel agents escaped with Crying Dawn Singer aboard the [[VR-10 Cuirilla-Rayl Xylines-class spacetug|CRX-Tug]] ''[[Worthless Fool]]''. {{Endgame}} Owen replaced Nak Farool with a [[Nile Owen's personal assistant|new personal assistant]]. This new aide later decided to desert and join the Alliance with the help of Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]. During this time, Owen's daughter was preparing her wedding, which would include influential guests from twenty different sectors. The Rebel [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] [[Aimi Loto]] had reprogrammed the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] of these visitors to malfunction and even attack [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] during the event—a task that took Loto several years. Cracken met with Loto, and she advised him to avoid protocol droids during the ceremony. In fact, the wedding ceremony was a hotbed of exploding, hostile and otherwise treacherous droids. Months before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Moff Owen sent a [[Company/Legends|company]] of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] from the [[First Sun Mobile Regiment]] to take over a Rebel outpost and provide him with [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] for questioning. Instead, the soldiers of fortune killed all the Rebel [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. Enraged, Owen executed his subordinates. This outraged the First Sun leaders, who began to distance themselves from the Empire for the first time since the Regiment's inception. Captain [[Zgorth'sth]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Department of Threat Assessment]] wrote about this event in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}}, republished as {{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/source20090511b.html?page=1|text=Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Prepare my shuttle. Our first priority is to locate Crying Dawn Singer. I must be the one who returns him to the Shashay. Then I will execute Braig Farool.|Moff Owen, to Admiral Sahreel|}} A very thin, middle-aged [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Moff Owen appeared threatening because of his smile and of the look in his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. A cultured man who excelled in management, he was commonly seen with the uniform of an Imperial officer. Owen added a furred cloak to the traditional clothes, as he was fond of this complement. His subordinates styled him "His Excellency." Owen was known to be intelligent, determined and cunning, driven only by his desire to serve the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] and to ambitiously advance his own career. While he intended to keep a firm control on Rayter sector, Owen was prone to outbursts of anger, during which he might literally kill anyone showing ineptitude or defiance, as shown when he killed Nak Farool or the First Sun mercenaries. ==Behind the scenes== Nile Owen first appeared in ''[[The Abduction]]'' ([[1992]]), a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[Chuck Truett]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. [[Allen Nunis]] drew several illustrations depicting Owen. Following his role-playing stats, Owen was knowledgeable in law enforcement and had been trained to use a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], but not to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] or to repair a starship. ===Alternate endings=== If the player characters of ''The Abduction'' leave KL-6TLF7V/T in Najarka, then Owen recovers it and his men access the droid's memory. This leads to Owen discovering the secret Rebel base at [[Berrol's Donn/Legends|Berrol's Donn]], and sending a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to obliterate it. If they can't escape Narg with Crying Dawn Singer, then Owen arrests the Shashay and tries to make him reveal the secret [[Galactic coordinates/Legends|galactic coordinates]] of Crytal Nest. Owen resorts to [[Torture/Legends|torture]], eventually killing Crying Dawn Singer. This outrages the Shashay leaders, who then refuse to help the Alliance. Losing such a powerful ally, the Alliance is unable to keep unity in the sector and fragments—something that cements Owen's power over Rayter. ==Appearances== *''[[The Abduction]]'' {{Reprint|The Abduction}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' *{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}} *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/source20090511b.html?page=1|text=Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}} {{C|Republished}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Owen, Nile}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy officers of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Moffs of the Galactic Empire]]
